verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is secreted by the epididymal epithelium into the epididymal lumen and binds post-translationally to the sperm surface during epididymal transit, where a tightly-bound population persists through capacitation and the acrosome reaction and is recognized by antibodies on ejaculated sperm (proposed contraceptive target). Surface display is restricted to the germline/sperm lineage, and attachment is acquired in a specialized secretory compartment with a wash-resistant pool. Best fits tissue-restricted surface display on sperm rather than a baseline somatic surface protein.
